summary: Small IF subsystem matching-improvement slice split from PR #2617. The final recorded diff changed src/melee/if/soundtest.c by giving the file-scope object un_803FA258 an explicit zero initializer. Review discussion also referenced attempted src/melee/if/textlib.c edits, but reviewer feedback objected to renaming variables and replacing them with defines; the final changed-files list only shows soundtest.c, suggesting those textlib changes were not part of the merged slice or were removed before merge.

key_files.reason: Only file in the final changed-files list; changed un_803FA258 from an uninitialized file-scope object declaration to an explicitly zero-initialized definition.
Mentioned in PR body and review comments, but not in the final changed-files list. Review feedback warned against variable renames and define-substitution patterns there.

what_changed: In src/melee/if/soundtest.c, the global/static aggregate-like object named un_803FA258 was changed from `} un_803FA258;` to `} un_803FA258 = { 0 };`.
The PR was created as an isolated path slice for `src/melee/if` from a larger matching-improvement branch, with the stated goal of making subsystem changes easier to review.
The PR body reported no broken matches or regressions in the source branch before splitting, and local `git diff --check` passed for the slice.
Review comments on src/melee/if/textlib.c criticized renaming variables and using defines in their place; those textlib edits are not present in the final one-file diff excerpt.
smart_moves: Kept the merged change extremely small: a one-line initializer adjustment in soundtest.c, reducing review and regression risk.

Used an explicit `{ 0 }` initializer on an otherwise anonymous/unmatched object, a common decomp tactic when the BSS/data placement or emitted object form depends on whether the symbol is treated as initialized.
Split the larger #2617 work by directory/subsystem, making IF-specific matching changes easier to validate independently.
Appears to have respected review pressure against speculative naming/define churn by leaving only the minimal soundtest.c change in the final recorded diff.
decomp_lessons: For file-scope objects in decomp work, an explicit zero initializer can matter for matching even when it is semantically equivalent in C; verify whether the target symbol belongs in the initialized-data form expected by the binary.

Do not rename poorly understood globals or replace names with defines solely for convenience. Reviewers prefer preserving provisional `un_...` symbols until there is stronger evidence for semantic names.
Small directory-sliced PRs are useful when a larger branch has many matching improvements; they isolate risk and simplify reviewer attention.
If a PR body lists files from the planned slice but the final diff contains fewer files, trust the final changed-files/diff as the evidence for what merged.
naming_conventions: The merged soundtest symbol stayed as `un_803FA258`, preserving the address-derived unknown-symbol naming convention.
Nearby symbols in the diff also use address-derived names such as `un_803FA348`, `un_803FA34C`, and `un_803FA32C`.
Review feedback explicitly discouraged speculative renaming of variables in textlib.c and replacing original variable names with defines.
When semantics are uncertain, keep `un_`-prefixed names rather than inventing descriptive names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use explicit zero initialization (`= { 0 }`) for aggregate/file-scope objects when needed to reproduce the original object emission or data-section behavior.
Prefer minimal source edits that target the exact mismatch instead of broader refactors in adjacent IF files.
Validate split branches with `git diff --check` and CI/decomp-dev reporting after extracting from a larger matching branch.

review_feedback.comment: Avoid renaming variables and using defines in their place.
review_feedback.lesson: Do not introduce speculative variable renames or define aliases in decomp cleanup without strong evidence.

review_feedback.comment: Same comment as above. Avoid renaming variables and using defines in their place.
review_feedback.lesson: The same naming/define concern applied to color/global-symbol edits in textlib.c.

follow_up_queries: Find other PRs where explicit `= { 0 }` initializers were used to fix matching or section placement.
Search for reviewer guidance from PsiLupan about avoiding speculative variable renames and define aliases.
Check subsequent IF/textlib.c PRs for accepted naming of devtext globals and GXColor globals.
Compare PR #2617 and its split PRs to see which IF matching changes were separated or reverted.
